original_abstract: This study explores the relationship between psychological maturity for marriage and emotional intelligence among young men. In the context of this research, marital maturity is conceptualized as a multi-dimensional construct comprising three core pillars: self-acceptance, internal integration (encompassing responsibility and a realistic worldview), and a constructive approach to marriage and family life. Utilizing data from a sample of 90 men aged 21–26, the study compared participants with high and low levels of marital readiness. The findings indicate that men with higher levels of psychological maturity for marriage demonstrate significantly greater emotional intelligence than their less mature peers. This link is primarily evidenced by a superior ability to identify, understand, and leverage emotional states to effectively navigate life challenges and interpersonal conflicts, suggesting that emotional competence is a vital foundation for building stable, long-term relationships.
humanized_abstract: Purpose: The objective of this study was to investigate the relationship between psychological marital maturity for young men and their emotional intelligence. Marital maturity as used in this study was considered as a multidimensional construct with three composing components: self-acceptance, internal integration (including responsibility and constructive cosmology) and a constructive marital and family attitude. For this purpose, in this study, the variables of marital maturity and emotional intelligence of 90 men, in the age range of 21 to 26 years, were examined through a comparative research method by describing the marital maturity and emotional intelligence of those men who had higher and lower marital readiness according to their scores. Results: The results of this research revealed that men with higher marital maturity had a higher level of emotional intelligence in comparison with those with lower marital maturity. The variables that described the relationship between the couples’ marital maturity and men’s emotional intelligence demonstrated that men with higher marital maturity could control and use their emotional intelligence more effectively and had a higher awareness of their emotional experiences. In addition, the men in this study had the ability to recognize, understand, and use their emotional experiences and the emotional experiences of others in the best way possible in their relationships and for dealing with various social issues and conflicts.
